seo.title: Flapjacks - not sure what it's called in Afrikaans 20 flapjacks - Marinda Kook

html:
Sometimes you're just in the mood for something different for breakfast or with your tea. It's quick and easy. How and with what you serve it makes all the difference.

  • 500 ml cake flour
  • 2 ml salt
  • 25 ml baking powder
  • 2 eggs
  • 250 ml sugar
  • 375ml milk

NOW:

  1. Sift the flour and baking powder together in a mixing bowl. Stir in the sugar.
  2. Whisk the eggs and milk together in another bowl. Then add it to your flour mixture.
  3. The big secret is that the mixture needs to rest a while before you cook it. Leave it aside for about 30 minutes. You can even make the mixture the night before if you want to use it for breakfast. Just keep it in the fridge.
  4. Heat oil in a pan. You can simply drop spoonfuls of batter into the pan, but if you really want to bake pretty flapjacks get yourself some egg rings and drop the batter into them. Once little holes form in the batter and it rises a bit, turn it over with a spatula.

You can serve your flapjacks with:

  • bacon
  • syrup
  • honey
  • fried banana and cinnamon
  • fresh berries
